attacking_style: Not a primary attacker; functions as sprint leadout and emergency finisher. When sprinting himself, emerges in final 1.5km from chaotic situations rather than planned launches.
team_tactics: Jumbo-Visma deploys him as selfless leadout domestique willing to sacrifice personal position. Team commits riders to chase/defend when he holds jersey but coordination can fail without external cooperation.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages and technical finales requiring classics-style bike handling. TTT strength shown winning stage by 20 seconds. Sprint stages with late positioning chaos suit his opportunism.
vulnerability: Relies on team numbers to defend leader responsibilities; isolated when rival teams refuse cooperation in complicated finales. Not a pure sprinter—opportunistic rather than dominant.
key_patterns: Acts as leadout man first, sprinter second—twice finished sprints after leadout duties (2 instances)
Demonstrates team-first mentality even in yellow, taking hard pulls 2km out risking jersey loss
Wins from chaos/crashes rather than organized sprints—both sprint results came from disrupted finales
Trek/Jumbo positions him through technical sections 1-2km out using classics positioning skills
Team defense of yellow jersey failed when rivals wouldn't share chase workload
form_trajectory: neutral: Fulfilled multiple roles successfully (TTT win, sprint wins, leadout work) but narrow July dataset shows tactical versatility rather than progression
